templates.title: Reduce Irrigation — Rain Expected
templates.message: Heavy rainfall is forecasted in the next 48 hours. Reduce or stop irrigation to prevent waterlogging. Ensure field drainage channels are clear.

templates.title: Increase Irrigation — Dry Spell
templates.message: No significant rainfall expected for the next 5 days. Soil moisture is declining. Schedule irrigation, preferably during early morning or late evening to minimize evaporation losses.

templates.title: Optimal Soil Moisture
templates.message: Current soil moisture levels are within the optimal range. Maintain current irrigation schedule. Monitor for the next 3 days.

templates.title: High Pest Risk — Humid Conditions
templates.message: High humidity and warm temperatures create favorable conditions for pest infestations (aphids, whiteflies, bollworms). Apply recommended pesticide spray. Use neem-based organic pesticides for eco-friendly control.

templates.title: Fungal Disease Alert
templates.message: Prolonged wet conditions increase risk of fungal diseases (rust, blight, mildew). Apply fungicide treatment and ensure proper crop spacing for air circulation.

templates.title: Ideal Sowing Window
templates.message: Monsoon onset confirmed with adequate soil moisture. This is the ideal window for sowing Kharif crops (Rice, Soybean, Corn). Prepare seedbeds and ensure seed treatment before sowing.

templates.title: Delay Sowing — Insufficient Moisture
templates.message: Soil moisture is below optimal levels for germination. Wait for adequate rainfall before sowing to ensure good seed-soil contact and germination rates.

templates.title: Harvest Before Rain
templates.message: If crops are at maturity stage, prioritize harvesting immediately. Heavy rain forecast can cause post-harvest losses including grain sprouting and quality deterioration.

templates.title: Good Harvesting Conditions
templates.message: Dry weather with moderate temperatures is ideal for harvesting and post-harvest drying. Proceed with harvest operations and ensure proper drying of grains to <14% moisture.

templates.title: Thunderstorm Warning
templates.message: Severe thunderstorms with strong winds (>50 km/h) and hail possible. Secure farm equipment, livestock shelters, and greenhouse structures. Avoid outdoor farm operations.

templates.title: Heat Stress Advisory
templates.message: Maximum temperatures exceeding 40°C expected. Provide shade and additional water for livestock. Young crop transplants may need shade nets. Avoid field work during peak hours (11AM-3PM).

templates.title: Apply Mulching
templates.message: High soil temperature and decreasing moisture detected. Apply organic mulch (straw, dried leaves) around crop bases to conserve soil moisture and regulate root zone temperature.

templates.title: Top-Dressing Fertilizer Application
templates.message: Adequate soil moisture and moderate conditions are suitable for fertilizer top-dressing. Apply nitrogen fertilizer (urea) in split doses. Avoid application before heavy rain to prevent nutrient leaching.
